nameMeanings.name: Wigton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Glasgow): habitational name from Wigton (Cumberland), named with the Old English personal name Wicga + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. It is unclear whether the Scottish name is of independent origin, but it does not seem to be associated with Wigtown (Wigtownshire).

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

en.lifeSummary: Beatrice Wigton was born in 1161, in Escrick, Yorkshire, England as the daughter of Elias Wigton of East Cowton. She had at least 7 sons and 3 daughters with Roger Lascelles II. She died in 1195, at the age of 34.

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrice Wigton(은)는 1161년 영국 요르크실르 스크릭에서 Elias Wigton of East Cowton의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Roger Lascelles II(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1195년 34세의 나이로 사망했다.
